title: rosa rubiginosa subvar. lactea
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subvar. lactea, also known as the sweet briar, is a subspecies of the Rosa rubiginosa plant, a species of flowering plant in the rose family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of salicylic acid. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa subvar. lactea include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
